raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I made several purchases from XXXX XXXX which included a mixer. I specifically informed the agent that I had electrical limitations which precludes me from using XXXX items. Imade a purchase for a XXXX XXXX XXXX that is XXXX as I do not have a XXXX XXXX XXXX current in my kitchen and I actually made that known to them. 

They shipped me a XXXX v instead of a XXXX v item. I tried severally to obtain a remediation number, which they would not provide. Based online reviews, this company perpetuates fraud against consumers by refusing to take back their nonconforming goods because they can count on incompetent and careless disregard by credit card companies that keep them in business and allows them to do this. 

I had to hire someone to put the mixer on a pallet, buy the shipping materials and told them they had XXXX days to send an Return Authorizations or RA or I will junk their product as I am not a storage facility. I run a bakery and space is limited. I charged just XXXX XXXX return fee for cost of hiring a handy man and materials and storage for XXXX  days since they charge XXXX XXXX restocking fee. 

Cost of equipment {$2400.00} Tax. {$250.00} Restocking fee. {$620.00} Total {$3300.00}. 

I then sent the dispute to Citibank and they kept requesteing the same information repeatedly. I had provided pictures of the invoice, the picture of the item that was shipped to show they sent the wrong product. Finally on XX/XX/year>, I was informed by Citicards, representative that the company is choosing not to return the amount, hence there is nothing they can do. 

What os the point of a dispute process? They expected the company to willingly without compulsion to do the right thing? This is insane. Where is the relief for the consumer? No wonder fraud is rampant because these fraudsters know they can count on companies like Citi to do the bare minimum and enable them in their fraud.
